message:
fix(security): harden listener PID hint file against symlink escape attacks with atomic write and prefix validation

New path_existing_prefix_has_symlink() walks every component of a path
checking via symlink_metadata whether any prefix is a symlink, also
rejecting paths containing .. (parent traversal).

New write_listener_pid_hint_atomic() replaces the old write logic with
a robust atomic-write approach: validates directory tree and target
path are real (not symlinks), creates uniquely-named temp file via
create_new(true) (up to 64 attempts), writes + sync_data, then renames
atomically. On failure, cleans up the temp file.

write_listener_pid_hint refactored to delegate to the atomic writer.
Directory permission lockdown (0o700) now happens after the atomic
write succeeds.

read_listener_pid_hint hardened: before reading, checks whether any
component of the hint path is a symlink and verifies via
symlink_metadata that the final path is a regular file (not a symlink
or special file).

Two new Unix-only tests:
- write_listener_pid_hint_rejects_symlinked_hint_directory
- read_listener_pid_hint_ignores_symlinked_hint_file

Eliminates TOCTOU vulnerabilities where an attacker could plant
symlinks in the PID hint directory to redirect writes to arbitrary
locations or trick reads into trusting attacker-controlled content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
